title: Jyväskylä Longitudinal Study of Personality and Social Development (JYLS): Personality Tests of 27-Year-Olds 1986
description: The data are part of the Jyväskylä Longitudinal Study of Personality and Social Development (JYLS), in which the same individuals have been followed over 30 years. At this research stage, 27-year-olds' different areas of life were under observation. The data were collected using a life situation questionnaire and a theme interview. In connection with the interview, the respondents completed two personality tests (Eysenck Personality Questionnaire (EPQ) and Sensation Seeking Scale (SSS)) included
license: In accordance with the agreement between FSD and the depositor.
